SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk

Class GameView

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.risk.GameView
Direct Known Subclasses:
Game
public class GameView extends Object
An immutable version of Risk.

This risk implementation is a fairly faithful implementation, according to Hasbro's Rules. Your main interactions will be through the IAgent interface. When it is your turn, the mutable Game instance will parse your Action and automatically update all internal game state on your behalf. If your action is illegal, your agent will be disqualified and replaced with a StaticAgent, which has no agency.

This assignment splits game states into 2 separate classes, the View, and the mutable instance. The mutable instance is a subclass of the view, which is convenient so you can call all the methods you want. We will always pass you a deep copy of the Game class, so you will not be able to modify the internal game state.

You can check if your version is an immutable snapshot by using 


  GameView game = null;  // put your game view object here
  if (game.getClass() == GameView.class) {
      System.out.println("Immutable snapshot");
  }

  // or similarly
  if (game instanceof Game mutableGame) {
      System.out.println("yup, pretty mutable");
  }

  • Constructor Details

    • GameView

      public GameView(Random random, Board board, List<IAgent> agents)
      Creates a new game view with the given board and agents.
      Parameters:
      random - The random number generator.
      board - The game board.
      agents - The list of agents, sorted by agent id.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if agents are not sorted in id order.

    • GameView

      public GameView(GameView copy1)
      Copy constructor.
      Parameters:
      copy1 - The game state to copy from.

  • Method Details

    • getRandom

      public Random getRandom()
      The random number generator used by this game instance.
      Returns:
      The non-null random number generator.

    • getBoard

      public Board getBoard()
      The board (e.g. the whole world) that holds all the territories, continents, etc.
      Returns:
      The non-null board.

    • getTerritoryOwners

      public Registry<TerritoryOwnerView> getTerritoryOwners()
      Stores who owns which territory.

      For each territory, there exists 1 territory owner instance to keep track of who owns it, and how many armies exist on it.

      Returns:
      The territory owners.

    • getAgents

      public List<IAgent> getAgents()
      The agents playing this game.

      Note that the default behavior is to return an immutable list (e.g. a list that throws an exception when you try to modify it). You will want a mutable Game instance if you want to modify this list.

      Note that in the snapshot of the game that you are passed, this method will ALWAYS return a list full of DummyAgent. These dummy agents are useless for actions (in fact, if you try to call DummyAgent.getAction(GameView, int, boolean), it will throw an UnsupportedOperationException). We return these dummy agents to prevent you from having access to the state of other agents, which would pose an unfair advantage.

      Returns:
      The list of agents (typically dummy agents).

    • getNumAgents

      public int getNumAgents()
      Returns the number of players in this game. Bounded between [2, 6] (inclusive).
      Returns:
      The number of agents playing the game.

    • getAgentInventory

      public List<TerritoryCard> getAgentInventory(int agentId)
      Returns the list of cards owned by the given agent.

      An agent is rewarded up to 1 territory card per turn for successfully claiming a territory by attack.

      Parameters:
      agentId - The agent idx whose inventory to grab.
      Returns:
      Their inventory.
      See Also:
      • TerritoryCard.hasValidTrade(List)

    • getNumPreviousRedemptions

      public int getNumPreviousRedemptions()
      The number of territory card trade-ins that have happened so far. At the start of the game, this is 0.

      Whenever an agent successfully redeems a set of cards, this counter will be incremented by 1. This number can be used to determine the next trade-in reward via: 

      
  GameView game = ...;
  int prev = game.getNumPreviousRedemptions();
  int bonusArmies = TerritoryCard.getRedemptionAmount(1 + prev);
      Returns:
      The number of trade-ins that have happened so far.

    • getDrawPile

      public SequencedCollection<TerritoryCard> getDrawPile()
      Returns the stack (LIFO) of territory cards.

      Note that this stack may become empty, so always check Collection.isEmpty() before trying to peek a value.

      Returns:
      The draw pile of territory cards.

    • getUnownedTerritories

      public List<Territory> getUnownedTerritories()
      Returns the list of all unowned territories. This method always returns an empty list, except during the initial game setup phase.
      Returns:
      The list of all unowned territories.

    • getTerritoriesOwnedBy

      public List<Territory> getTerritoriesOwnedBy(int agentIdx)
      Returns the list of all territories owned by a specific agent.
      Parameters:
      agentIdx - The unique idx of the agent.
      Returns:
      The territories owned by this agent.

    • getContinentsOwnedBy

      public List<Continent> getContinentsOwnedBy(int agentIdx)
      Returns the list of all continents owned by a specific agent.
      Parameters:
      agentIdx - The unique idx of the agent.
      Returns:
      The continents owned by the agent.

    • getNumTurns

      public int getNumTurns()
      The number of turns played total, where 1 turn is the set of actions 1 player takes before passing to the next player.
      Returns:
      The number of turns played.

    • getArmyBudgets

      public int[] getArmyBudgets()
      The budgets available to each agent, indexed by IAgent.agentId().

      Intended for internal use. The array will almost always be full of zeroes, except at the start of a player's turn or when they redeem cards.

      Returns:
      The army budgets for all agents.

    • getBonusArmiesFor

      public int getBonusArmiesFor(int agentIdx)
      Returns the number of bonus armies an agent will receive at the start of their turn.

      This is DIFFERENT then getArmyBudgets(), which is almost always 0 (since agents are required to place down armies as soon as they receive the armies). Instead, this number is an estimation on how many armies you will be rewarded.

      Parameters:
      agentIdx - The agent to estimate for.
      Returns:
      The armies to receive.

    • isOver

      public boolean isOver()
      Returns true if the game is over (only 1 player owns territories).
      Returns:
      Whether the game has reached a terminal state.
